note_data.title: Trigonometry
keyPoints: Natural numbers are all the positive integers from 1 to infinity, fractions or decimals are not included
Whole numbers are all the positive integers including zero
Integers include positive numbers, negative numbers or zero and are the most common word used by the examiner
Prime numbers have exactly 2 factors: 1 and the number itself
Composite numbers have more than two factors
Rational numbers can be expressed in the form of a ratio and must either end (terminating decimals) or repeat (recurring decimals)
Irrational numbers cannot be expressed in the form of a fraction, such as π, √2, √7
Real numbers include all rational and irrational numbers - all the numbers that exist

HCF (Highest common factor) is the largest number that is a common factor of two different numbers, while LCM (Lowest common multiple) is the smallest number that is the multiple of two different numbers

definitions.definition: All the positive integers from 1 to infinity. Fractions or decimals are not included. They are also called Positive Integers
Numbers that have exactly 2 factors: 1 and the number itself
Any whole number that can be written as a pure fraction or decimal. Numbers that can be expressed in the form of a ratio
An organised, unique collection of elements. You are not allowed to repeat the same element in a set

howToAnswer.instruction: Show all working steps, identify which trigonometric ratio or rule to use, substitute values correctly
Draw a clear labelled diagram, identify the required triangle, apply appropriate trigonometric method
Work towards the given answer, showing each algebraic step clearly

modelAnswer.question: A ship sails 12 km on a bearing of 040° from port P to point Q, then 8 km on a bearing of 120° to point R. Calculate the distance PR.
modelAnswer.answer: Draw triangle PQR. Angle QPR = 120° - 40° = 80°. Using cosine rule: PR² = PQ² + QR² - 2(PQ)(QR)cos(80°). PR² = 12² + 8² - 2(12)(8)cos(80°). PR² = 144 + 64 - 192 × 0.174 = 174.6. PR = √174.6 = 13.2 km
markBreakdown: Mark 2: Correct application of cosine rule

commonMistakes: Including fractions or decimals when listing natural numbers
Confusing integers (which include negative numbers) with natural numbers or whole numbers
Repeating the same element in a set when sets must contain unique elements only
Confusing HCF and LCM - HCF is the largest common factor while LCM is the smallest common multiple
examinerTips: Integers are the most common word used by the examiner
Candidates are required to memorise square numbers up till 15 for Paper 1
A colon ':' in set notation should be read as 'such that'
formulas: sin θ = opp/hyp, cos θ = adj/hyp, tan θ = opp/adj
Sine rule: a/sin A = b/sin B = c/sin C
Cosine rule: a² = b² + c² - 2bc cos A
note_data.keyDiagramDescription: Right-angled triangle clearly labelled with hypotenuse, opposite and adjacent sides relative to the angle being used, with angle of elevation/depression marked from horizontal line
pastPaperRefs: This topic was tested in M/J 2023 P2 Q8 (6 marks) — they asked students to find the height of a building using angle of elevation and horizontal distance
This topic was tested in O/N 2022 P1 Q12 (4 marks) — students had to solve a trigonometric equation sin(2x) = 0.5 for 0° ≤ x ≤ 180°
This topic was tested in M/J 2022 P2 Q6 (5 marks) — calculate the area of triangle ABC using the formula ½ab sin C
This topic was tested in O/N 2023 P1 Q15 (3 marks) — find exact values of sin 60°, cos 45°, and tan 30° without calculator

workedExample.question: A ladder of length 8m leans against a vertical wall. The foot of the ladder is 3m from the base of the wall. Calculate the angle between the ladder and the ground. (4 marks)
steps: Step 1: Draw a right-angled triangle with hypotenuse = 8m (ladder), adjacent = 3m (ground distance)
Step 2: Identify which trigonometric ratio to use: cos θ = adjacent/hypotenuse
Step 4: Find the angle: θ = cos⁻¹(0.375) = 67.98° ≈ 68.0°
workedExample.answer: The angle between the ladder and the ground is 68.0°
workedExample.markScheme: 1 mark for correct trigonometric ratio, 1 mark for substitution, 1 mark for calculation, 1 mark for final answer to 3 significant figures

examinerWarnings: Examiner Report 2023: 'Many candidates confused sine and cosine ratios in right-angled triangles' — always draw and label the triangle clearly, identifying opposite, adjacent and hypotenuse relative to the given angle
Examiner Report 2022: 'Students frequently forgot to check their calculator was in degree mode' — always verify calculator settings and state units in your final answer
Examiner Report 2023: 'Common error was using wrong triangle in bearings questions' — when solving bearing problems, always draw North lines and identify the angle being calculated clearly
note_data.keyDiagram: Draw a right-angled triangle clearly labeled with: hypotenuse (longest side opposite the right angle), opposite side (opposite to the angle being used), adjacent side (next to the angle being used). Mark the angle with an arc and label it θ or the given letter. Include the trigonometric ratios: SOH (sin = opp/hyp), CAH (cos = adj/hyp), TOA (tan = opp/adj) written beside the triangle.
note_data.quickRecap: Numbers are classified into different families including natural numbers (positive integers), whole numbers (including zero), integers (positive, negative or zero), and real numbers (all numbers that exist). Prime numbers have exactly two factors while composite numbers have more than two. Sets are organised collections of unique elements with specific notation symbols for operations like union and intersection.
